extraction.one_line_summary: DEMARE Arnaud won from chaos after Ackerman crashed 1.2km out, exploiting Viviani's reliance on Caleb Ewan's wheel selection.
tactical_patterns: Viviani shadowed Caleb Ewan's wheel from 5-6km out instead of positioning independently, losing tactical flexibility when sprint fractured.
Lotto-Soudal deliberately let Dorthea solo 2.3km knowing exact power calculations to reel him precisely at flamme rouge.
Groupama-FDJ positioned Demare independently left side while rivals clustered right, avoiding the barriers crash at 1.2km.

rider_signals.ACKERMAN Pascal: negative: crashed 1.2km out when leading sprinters clipped barriers left side, eliminated from sprint contention
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: second place again despite perfect positioning reveals inability to win without dominant leadout train
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: positive: first 2019 win shows strength returning, critically positioned left independently while chaos erupted right barriers
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: carried Viviani's wheel from 6km but couldn't convert, suggesting leadership burden without finishing power
rider_signals.DORTHEA Ramblin: neutral: late solo attack 2.3km out calculated by Lotto power data, never realistically threatening organized sprint teams
